records.name: HOPE HEALTH (Hope Faith Homeless Assistance Campus)
records.free_claim: Hope Health is the campus Community Wellness Clinic 'designed to provide FREE wellness care to all' and, with partnering groups, provides guests 'free medical, dental, eye, auditory, chiropractic, behavioral, telemedicine, and prescriptions all under one roof.' No charge to patients.
records.nonprofit_claim: Hope Faith is a non-denominational, Christian-based 501(c)(3) nonprofit (est. 2004), Kansas City's only low-barrier homeless assistance campus; displays Candid Platinum nonprofit seal. Dental care is a charitable service for people experiencing homelessness/at-risk.
records.what: Free dental care offered on-site as part of a free community wellness clinic at a faith-based homeless services nonprofit. Serves uninsured/homeless and at-risk adults at no cost.

records.name: JayDoc Free Clinic (Dental)
records.free_claim: Named the JayDoc 'Free' Clinic; dental services provided at no cost. Per clinic info, dental is by-appointment on alternating Tuesday evenings and 'all extractions are free.' No charge to uninsured/underinsured patients.
records.nonprofit_claim: Charitable student-run free clinic serving the uninsured and underinsured of Greater Kansas City; volunteer-staffed, no patient fees. (Not a fee-charging dental-school clinic — care is donated and free.)
records.what: Volunteer free dental clinic (currently limited to free tooth extractions) by referral/appointment for uninsured and underinsured adults. Email jaydocfreedentalclinic@gmail.com to request an appointment.

records.name: Donated Dental Services (DDS) — Dental Lifeline Network of Missouri
records.free_claim: Volunteer dentists provide 'comprehensive treatment to eligible patients' at no cost; applicants must have 'no means to afford dental care.' Care donated free in volunteer dentists' own offices (no cosmetic care).
records.nonprofit_claim: Dental Lifeline Network is a national 501(c)(3) charitable nonprofit; DDS is its donated-care program staffed by volunteer dentists and labs.
records.what: By-application program providing free comprehensive donated dental care to people who are elderly (65+), permanently disabled, or medically compromised and cannot afford care. Limited to participating Missouri counties. Coordinator Francisca Villa.

records.name: Donated Dental Services (DDS) — Dental Lifeline Network of Kansas
records.free_claim: Volunteer dentists 'provide treatment at no cost to eligible patients'; applicants must have 'no means to afford dental care.' Comprehensive care donated free (no cosmetic care).
records.nonprofit_claim: Dental Lifeline Network is a national 501(c)(3) charitable nonprofit; DDS in Kansas is also supported by the Kansas Dental Association/charitable foundation network of volunteer dentists and labs.
records.what: By-application program providing free comprehensive donated dental care to Kansas residents who are elderly (65+), permanently disabled, or medically compromised and cannot afford care. Limited to participating counties. Coordinator Anita Garbo.

records.name: Kansas Mission of Mercy (KMOM)
records.free_claim: Large-scale free dental clinic providing 'free oral healthcare to patients of all ages who cannot otherwise afford or access care.' Volunteer dentists/hygienists provide millions of dollars of free fillings, extractions, and cleanings. 'No dental records or proof of income are needed.'
records.nonprofit_claim: Project of the Kansas Dental Charitable Foundation, a 501(c)(3) organization, with the Kansas Dental Association. Care donated by volunteers.
records.what: Annual large-scale free dental event (no fixed walk-in address). Returns to Kansas City, KS on January 29-30, 2027 at the Homefield KCK Showcase Center. Walk-up free care for all ages, first-come basis.

records.name: Missouri Mission of Mercy (MOMOM)
records.free_claim: Large-scale dental clinic where 'hundreds of volunteer dentists and dental allied professionals donate time and supplies to perform free dental care.' Since 2011, volunteers have provided $7.98M+ in free care to 12,311 patients at no cost.
records.nonprofit_claim: Organized by the MDA Foundation, the charitable 501(c)(3) subsidiary/foundation of the Missouri Dental Association; care fully donated by volunteers.
records.what: Annual statewide free dental event (no fixed walk-in address); location varies by year and applications open ahead of each event. Serves Missouri residents (including KC metro) who cannot afford or access dental care.

records.name: APPNA Clinic (Association of Physicians of Pakistani-Descent of North America, St. Louis)
records.what: Walk-in community clinic providing free dental checkups and extractions (plus medical and eye care) to all patients regardless of insurance status. Staffed by volunteer licensed dentists.
records.free_claim: Org site states: "Providing services free of cost irrespective of race, religion, nationality or insurance status" and "a non-profit clinic dedicated to providing free healthcare to the community." Dental services include "Dental checkup and extractions."
records.nonprofit_claim: Identified on its own site as a "Non-Profit 501(c)(3)" organization (St. Louis Chapter of APPNA with the Islamic Foundation of Greater St. Louis).

records.name: Rural Parish Mobile Dental Clinic (Archdiocese of St. Louis)
records.what: Mobile (no fixed walk-in address) free, faith-based dental clinic of the Catholic Archdiocese of St. Louis serving uninsured rural adults ages 19-65 at or below 200% federal poverty level. Provides exams, x-rays, cleanings, fillings, extractions, and dentures. Serves Franklin and Jefferson counties (plus Washington, Lincoln, St. Francois, Ste. Genevieve). Schedule by contacting the clinic.
records.free_claim: Org site footer: "Free mobile care for uninsured rural Missourians"; describes "free, faith-based medical and dental care" delivered "at no cost to income-qualifying adults without insurance."
records.nonprofit_claim: Operated by the Rural Parish Clinic of the Catholic Archdiocese of St. Louis (faith-based charitable program; listed on igivecatholic.org and archstl.org).

records.name: Donated Dental Services (DDS) - Dental Lifeline Network of Missouri
records.what: By-application (no walk-in address) statewide program providing free comprehensive dental treatment via volunteer dentists/labs to people who cannot afford care and are elderly (65+), permanently disabled, or medically fragile/needing medically necessary care. Covers St. Louis City and many eastern MO counties incl. Franklin. Waitlist can be several months to over a year.
records.free_claim: Program page: "The program provides free dental care through volunteer dentists and laboratories"; "Volunteers provide comprehensive treatment to eligible patients" at no cost.
records.nonprofit_claim: "Dental Lifeline Network is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit registered in the US under EIN: 84-6129064."

records.name: Missouri Mission of Mercy (MOMOM) - MDA Foundation
records.what: Annual large-scale free dental clinic event (no fixed walk-in address; location/date varies by year) providing free oral healthcare to patients of all ages who cannot afford or access care. Run by the MDA Foundation, the charitable subsidiary of the Missouri Dental Association. Next event date/location not yet announced as of mid-2026; check modental.org.
records.free_claim: MDA Foundation page: MOMOM is "a large-scale dental clinic that provides free oral healthcare to patients of all ages who cannot otherwise afford or access care" ($7.98M in free care to 12,311 patients since 2011).
records.nonprofit_claim: Run by the MDA Foundation, described as "MDA's charitable not-for-profit subsidiary."

records.name: Hannibal Free Clinic
records.what: Non-profit volunteer free clinic serving uninsured, low-income adults; provides free dental extractions (non-emergency, scheduled care) alongside primary medical care. All services delivered by volunteer providers.
records.free_claim: Provides 'free, non-emergency, scheduled primary health care' and lists 'dental extractions' among services provided without charge to uninsured, low-income patients.
records.nonprofit_claim: Described as 'a non-profit community charity' where 'all services are provided by volunteer physicians, nurses and support staff'; governed by a volunteer board representing faith-based organizations, healthcare providers, and civic groups.

records.name: Donated Dental Services (DDS) — Dental Lifeline Network of Missouri
records.what: By-application statewide program providing free comprehensive dental treatment via volunteer dentists/labs to vulnerable adults. No walk-in location. Eligibility: no means to pay AND (age 65+, permanently disabled, or medically necessary dental care). Accepts applications from counties covering the target area including Boone (Columbia), Cole (Jefferson City), Marion (Hannibal), and Knox/Scotland/Randolph (Kirksville region). Waitlist-based.
records.free_claim: 'Volunteers provide comprehensive treatment to eligible patients' at no cost; applicants must have 'no financial means for dental care.' Confirmed by Love Columbia resource page as 'No cost to qualifying patients.'
records.nonprofit_claim: Dental Lifeline Network is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization; care is donated by volunteer dentists and dental laboratories.

records.name: Missouri Mission of Mercy (MOMOM) — MDA Foundation
records.what: Large-scale periodic free dental clinic event providing free oral healthcare to patients of all ages who cannot afford or access care. No fixed walk-in address; held as periodic/announced events at varying Missouri locations. No 2026 date currently announced (foundation states 'no definite plans to announce' at time of check).
records.free_claim: MOMOM provides 'free oral healthcare to patients of all ages who cannot otherwise afford or access care'; since 2011 volunteers have provided over $7.98 million in free care to 12,311 patients.
records.nonprofit_claim: Run by the MDA Foundation, described as the Missouri Dental Association's 'charitable not-for-profit subsidiary' established in 1977; care delivered by dental and general volunteers.

records.name: The Tooth Truck (Ronald McDonald House Charities of the Ozarks)
records.what: Mobile dental office providing a full range of free dental care (x-rays, cleanings, sealants, fillings, root canals, extractions) to children ages 3-18 who qualify for free/reduced school lunch. Serves Springfield, Billings, Clever, Republic, Walnut Grove, Willard. Children only.
records.free_claim: Org site: 'Any child between the ages of 3 and 18 that qualifies for the free/reduced school lunch program is eligible to receive free treatment on the Tooth Truck, regardless of whether or not you have Medicaid coverage. There is no charge for services provided by the Tooth Truck as it operates entirely with donations from the community and Missouri Medicaid reimbursements.'
records.nonprofit_claim: Operated by Ronald McDonald House Charities of the Ozarks, Inc. (501(c)(3) nonprofit, EIN 43-1371143) jointly with The Tooth Truck, Inc.; funded by community donations.

records.name: Community Clinic of Southwest Missouri
records.what: Volunteer-staffed, donation-funded community clinic in Joplin offering free adult dental services (evaluation, extraction, cleanings) by appointment when volunteer providers are available. A $10 voluntary donation is requested but services are stated to be free.
records.free_claim: Org site (dental page): 'We are 100% funded by donations, grants, small businesses and fundraisers. So even though our services are FREE, we ask for a $10.00 donation to help keep them that way.' Dental services 'Provided as volunteer providers are available.' Note: $10 is framed as a requested donation, not a mandatory fee.
records.nonprofit_claim: Non-profit community clinic, '100% funded by donations, grants, small businesses and fundraisers'; care delivered by volunteer providers.

records.name: Missouri Mission of Mercy (MOMOM)
records.what: Large-scale annual free dental clinic run by the Missouri Dental Association / MDA Foundation. Set up with portable equipment in a public arena; provides free dental screenings, cleanings, fillings, extractions, etc. to patients of all ages on a first-come basis. No fixed walk-in address; date/location varies year to year (no 2026 event announced as of this research).
records.free_claim: MDA Foundation: 'The Missouri Mission of Mercy (MOMOM) is a large-scale dental clinic that provides free oral healthcare to patients of all ages who cannot otherwise afford or access care.' Since 2011, volunteers have provided over $7.98 million in free care to 12,311 patients.
records.nonprofit_claim: Project of the MDA Foundation, a 501(c)(3) charitable subsidiary of the Missouri Dental Association; staffed by volunteer dentists and general volunteers.

records.name: Donated Dental Services (DDS) - Dental Lifeline Network of Missouri
records.what: By-application program connecting eligible patients with volunteer dentists and dental labs for comprehensive free dental treatment. Eligibility: cannot afford care AND are 65+, permanently disabled, or medically fragile. No emergency or cosmetic care. No walk-in address; statewide including the Ozarks. MO DDS Coordinator: Francisca Villa, 573-636-4440.
records.free_claim: Dental Lifeline Network: 'Patients may qualify for free dental treatment through Donated Dental Services (DDS)... Volunteers provide comprehensive treatment to eligible patients' at no cost. Care is provided through a volunteer network of dentists and dental labs.
records.nonprofit_claim: Dental Lifeline Network is a national 501(c)(3) nonprofit founded in 1974; care donated by volunteer dentists and labs.

records.name: Kansas Mission of Mercy (KMOM)
records.what: Annual large-scale 2-day free dental clinic (cleanings, fillings, extractions; no dentures) open to patients of all ages, first-come first-served, no income/residency/eligibility requirements. Rotates to a different Kansas host city each year (recent hosts include Manhattan 2026, Salina 2024, Topeka 2023; next event Kansas City, KS Jan 29-30, 2027). No fixed walk-in address — location changes annually. Run by the Kansas Dental Charitable Foundation.
records.free_claim: 'provides free oral healthcare to patients of all ages who cannot otherwise afford or access care'; org page states services and meals provided 'at no charge'; 'No dental records or proof of income are needed.' Supported entirely by donations/grants; no state or federal funding.
records.nonprofit_claim: Project of the Kansas Dental Charitable Foundation, a 501(c)(3) organization, in partnership with the Kansas Dental Association.

records.name: Missouri Mission of Mercy (MOMOM)
records.what: Annual large-scale free dental clinic providing free oral healthcare (cleanings, fillings, extractions) to patients of all ages who cannot otherwise afford or access care. Two-day events that rotate to a different Missouri host city each year; first-come, first-served, no residency requirement. As of 2026 no upcoming date/location announced. No fixed walk-in address.
records.free_claim: 'a large-scale dental clinic that provides free oral healthcare to patients of all ages who cannot otherwise afford or access care.' Since 2011 over 8,300 volunteers delivered more than $7.98 million in free care to ~12,311 patients.
records.nonprofit_claim: Organized by the MDA Foundation, the charitable nonprofit subsidiary of the Missouri Dental Association.

records.name: Kansas Donated Dental Services (DDS) — Dental Lifeline Network
records.what: By-application program providing free, comprehensive dental treatment in volunteer dentists' own offices statewide (including the Topeka, Manhattan, Lawrence, and Salina areas). Eligibility: no financial means for care AND one of — over 65, permanently disabled, or needs medically necessary dental care. Apply online or by mail; not a walk-in clinic.
records.free_claim: 'Volunteers provide comprehensive treatment at no cost to eligible patients.' Comprehensive care donated by volunteer dentists in their own offices.
records.nonprofit_claim: Dental Lifeline Network is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit, EIN 84-6129064 (DDS run with the Kansas Dental Association).

records.name: Missouri Donated Dental Services (DDS) — Dental Lifeline Network
records.what: By-application program providing free, comprehensive dental treatment in volunteer dentists' own offices in participating Missouri counties (currently 31 counties; verify NW Missouri / St. Joseph–Buchanan County coverage at application). Eligibility: financial need AND one of — over 65, permanently disabled, or needs medically necessary care. Apply online or by mail; no walk-in address.
records.free_claim: 'the program provides donated services by volunteer dentists at no cost to eligible patients.'
records.nonprofit_claim: Dental Lifeline Network is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit, EIN 84-6129064.

records.name: Kansas Mission of Mercy (KMOM)
records.what: Annual large-scale free dental clinic (a traveling 2-day event that rotates among Kansas cities). Volunteer dentists and hygienists provide free cleanings, fillings, and extractions to patients of all ages. No eligibility, income, or residency requirements; first come, first served. Serves Central/Western Kansas residents when held in or near the region (e.g., held in Hutchinson Jan 2025, Hays 2022, Wichita prior years).
records.free_claim: Provides free oral healthcare to patients of all ages who cannot otherwise afford or access care. Volunteer dentists and hygienists provide millions of dollars' worth of free dental work such as fillings, extractions and dental cleanings. No dental records or proof of income needed. Support comes entirely from donations and grants; uses no state or federal funding.
records.nonprofit_claim: A project of the Kansas Dental Charitable Foundation, a 501(c)(3) organization, and the Kansas Dental Association. Run by the charitable foundation since 2003; staffed by volunteer dentists/hygienists.

records.name: Donated Dental Services (DDS) — Dental Lifeline Network of Kansas
records.what: By-application program providing free comprehensive dental treatment to people who are elderly (65+), have a permanent disability, or are medically fragile and lack income to pay for care. Volunteer dentists and labs donate treatment. Statewide program covering Central/Western Kansas counties (note: some counties periodically have waitlists). Does not provide emergency or cosmetic care.
records.free_claim: Provides access to dental care for people who cannot afford it and have a permanent disability, or are elderly (65+), or medically fragile. Volunteers provide comprehensive treatment to eligible patients at no charge; applicants must lack adequate income to pay for dental care.
records.nonprofit_claim: Dental Lifeline Network is a national nonprofit humanitarian organization founded in 1974; DDS in Kansas is a project initiated with the Kansas Dental Association. Care is donated by volunteer dentists and labs.
